comments.body: this run-on is kinda confusing. replace with something like "This need not be the case though because the source of this variation can be pinned down precisely. It results from the underlying variation in the set of tasks that make up the benchmark."

comments.name: Celia
comments.body:
some comma splicing and run-on sentences that could be tightened up in here. e.g.

"It should be clear how this distribution could help with score forecasting. In the first instance, the distribution is intrinsic to the benchmark, so we wouldn't have to wait for models to score highly in order to understand the benchmark's shape (in theory — in practice it's slightly harder). Additionally, it enables us to predict sudden rushes or slowdowns before they happen, or diagnose them after they occur. This is because a change can be one of two things: (1) a genuine step change or discontinuity in model capabilities, or (2) an unnatural grouping of task difficulties that all get surpassed at once. Disambiguating between these is hard at present; difficulty-oriented benchmarking offers a solution."
comments.quote: It should be clear how this distribution could help score forecasting, in the first instance, it’s intrinsic to the benchmark and so we wouldn’t have to wait for models to score highly to understand the shape of this benchmark (in theory, in practice it’s slightly harder). Additionally, it enables us to predict sudden rushes or slows before they happen, or diagnose them after they happened. This is because a change can be one of two things (1) genuine step changes and discontinuities in model capabilities (2) unnatural groupings of task difficulties which all get surpassed at once. Disambiguating this is at present hard, difficulty-oriented benchmarking offers a solution.

Isn't the sigmoid meant to be fitted to the frontier trend?
